attributes.description: Alerts when any HTTP sessions are rejected (`Catalina_Manager_rejectedSessions` increases). Rejection means `maxActiveSessions` was reached — real users are being turned away.
attributes.name: [Tomcat OTel] Session rejection

investigation_guide.blob:
## Tomcat session rejection

### What fired
`Catalina_Manager_rejectedSessions` increased on at least one web application during the evaluation window.

### Why it matters
Sessions are rejected when the manager reaches `maxActiveSessions`. Every rejection is a user that cannot establish a session — likely a login failure or lost shopping cart. Silent session rejection often goes unnoticed until support tickets arrive.

### Triage
1. Sessions & Applications dashboard: find the affected context.
2. Compare `activeSessions` vs `maxActiveSessions` and the rate of `sessionCreateRate` / `sessionExpireRate`.
3. Check for unusually long session TTLs or a recent traffic spike.

### Remediation
- Raise `maxActiveSessions` (verify heap can tolerate it).
- Shorten session TTL or promote session expiration.
- Investigate why sessions accumulate (leaked references, absent logout flows).

### Tuning
- Fires on any rejection. Lower sensitivity is rarely appropriate since every rejection is a user-visible failure.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-tomcat.otel-*
| WHERE Catalina_Manager_rejectedSessions IS NOT NULL
| STATS
    max_rejected = MAX(Catalina_Manager_rejectedSessions),
    min_rejected = MIN(Catalina_Manager_rejectedSessions)
  BY host.name, attributes.context, attributes.host
| EVAL new_rejections = max_rejected - min_rejected
// Any session rejection means maxActiveSessions has been reached
// This directly impacts users who cannot establish new sessions
| WHERE new_rejections > 0
| SORT new_rejections DESC
